steps.title: Eppendorf Safe-Lock microcentrifuge tube with tissue...
steps.instruction: Eppendorf Safe-Lock microcentrifuge tube with tissue sample and glass ball (6 mm) freezes at -80°C, grind in the MM300 Mixer Mill for 2 min at 30 Hz. Alternatively, grind the sample in the lysis solution, so that there is enough empty space in the tube.

steps.title: In a 2 ml tube with...
steps.instruction: In a 2 ml tube with mechanically disrupted tissue/seeds/leaves/herbarium or DNA solution (CTAB purification) add 1 ml CTAB solution buffer with RNAse A (the sample mass should not exceed 100 mg), vortex thoroughly, and incubate the samples at 55-65°C during 30-120 min or longer (long incubation increases DNA yield). 02:00:00 Undetermined

steps.title: Optionally, spin at maximum speed in...
steps.instruction: Optionally, spin at maximum speed in a microcentrifuge for 2 minutes, and preferably transfer the entire clarified supernatant to a new 2 ml Safe-Lock microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Transfer the entire clarified supernatant to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire clarified supernatant to a new 2 ml microcentrifuge tube contains an equal volume of chloroform. Mix well for 3-5 minutes in the MM300 Mixer Mill at 30 Hz.

steps.title: Add to the tube an equal volume of chloroform
steps.instruction: Add to the tube an equal volume of chloroform. Mix well for 1-5 minutes in the MM300 Mixer Mill at 30 Hz or vortex thoroughly for 1 minute. Spin at maximum speed in a microcentrifuge for 1-3 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer the entire clarified upper aqueous...
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire clarified upper aqueous layer to a new 2 ml microcentrifuge tube which contains half to an equal volume of 2-propanol and vortex thoroughly (5-10 secs).

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed in a microcentrifuge for 2-5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed in a microcentrifuge for 2-5 minutes. A whitish DNA pellet should be visible.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and wash the pellet by adding 1
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and wash the pellet by adding 1.5-1.8 ml 70% ethanol, vortex thoroughly. At this stage, DNA samples can be stored at room temperature or refrigerated.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 2...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 2 min and carefully discard the supernatant by decanting or with a micropipette. A whitish DNA pellet should be visible during discarding a supernatant.

steps.title: Do not dry the DNA pellet...
steps.instruction: Do not dry the DNA pellet and immediately dissolve it in 300 μl 1xTE, pH 8.0 at 55°C for 10-20 minutes.
